name: Roasted Corn, Red Pepper & Shiitake Mushroom
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Place a whole large head of garlic directly on the rack and dry roast for 40 minutes. Remove the garlic head and allow it to cool for 1 hour.
3. Turn the garlic head on its side and slice it in half to expose the cloves. Carefully remove the soft meat of the cloves and turn them out onto a cutting board. Chop into a paste. Reserve.
4. Heat 2 tablespoons butter in a large sauté pan. Add the mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes on medium low heat.
5. Add onion, corn, jalapeno, and roasted peppers. Cook slowly for 10 minutes.
6. In a large mixing bowl, beat eggs; add egg white. Beat thoroughly.
7. Add buttermilk, 3/4 cup of the grated cheese, heavy cream, garlic paste, cilantro, and seasonings. Add the cooked vegetable mixture. Mix well.
8. Grease a bottom and sides of an ovenproof oval or rectangular baking pan (9-inch by 1-inch) with remaining butter.
9. Line the bottom of the pan with 3 corn tortillas all the way to the edges.
10. Place half of the corn-mushroom mix over the tortillas.
11. Repeat layers with the remaining ingredients.
12. Top mixture with remaining grated cheese.
13. Bake in a 350-degree oven for 35 minutes or until thoroughly set. Remove and allow to cool 15 minutes before unmolding to slice, or serve directly out of the baking dish.
Servings: 6 to 8 as a side dish.
